title: UDLM Provider — Capability Declaration (adopted standards + topology/mobility/operational/sovereignty)
description: The provider-authored capability declaration (adopted-standards.md §3.2, DCM ADS-001; UDLM ADR-004). Carries (a) `adopted_standard_support` — which versions of each adopted external standard the provider can emit/consume — and (b) `capabilities`: the provider's declared, VERSIONED, accreditable capabilities. A capability is the accreditation/version unit (its `capability_uuid` + `version` is what an accreditation binds to, governance/accreditation-and-authorization-matrix.md §3.3.1) and may realize MORE THAN ONE `(verb × domain)` category (ADR-PROV-002); topology/mobility/operational/sovereignty vary per category WITHIN it (ADR-004 §2 — the accreditable unit is coarser than the per-category grain). Per-category blocks OVERRIDE `provider_defaults` (finest-granularity-wins, ADR-004 §0/§4). This is DATA; matching/negotiation and the 1-1 claim↔accreditation reconciliation are Policy (DCM).

uuid.description: RFC 9562 v4 identity of the provider — the `subject_uuid` an accreditation attests against (accreditation §3.3.1 axis 1).

kind.description: Provider type (provider-contract.md). A provider may emit adopted-standard data in its Information-Provider role even when it also provisions (service).

adopted_standard_support.description: One entry per adopted standard this provider supports.

provider_defaults.description: Provider-level DEFAULT blocks (UDLM ADR-004 §0/§4). Any per-category block on a capability OVERRIDES these for that category (finest-granularity-wins). Optional — a provider whose whole offering shares one stance sets it here; a multi-category provider overrides per category.

conformance_claims.description: Provider-wide DECLARED standards adherence (self-declared). A per-capability entry may add/override; each claim is trusted only when an accreditation attests it (§3.7).

capabilities.description: The provider's declared, versioned, accreditable capabilities (UDLM ADR-004). Each is the accreditation/version unit and realizes one or more `(verb × domain)` categories.

standard.description: External standard name, e.g. FOCUS, OpenCost, OSCAL, SCIM.

supports.description: The version range/pins the provider can serve — a pin ("1.4"), wildcard ("1.x"), or range (">=1.2 <2.0"). RANGE RESOLUTION is Policy (DCM ADS-003), not the schema.

preferred.description: The concrete version the provider prefers within `supports`.

direction.description: Whether the provider produces the standard's data, consumes it, or both.

capability.description: One declared, VERSIONED, accreditable capability — the accreditation/version unit (ADR-004 §5; accreditation §3.3.1 grain 3 binds `capability_uuid` + `version`). It realizes one or more `(verb × domain)` categories; topology/mobility/operational/sovereignty are per-category, WITHIN this capability.

capability_uuid.description: Stable RFC 9562 v4 identity of this capability — the anchor an accreditation binds to (accreditation §3.3.1).

version.description: Immutable-once-published version (VERSIONING.md). Bumped on ANY change to its attested surface (any category's topology/mobility/operational/sovereignty/resource_types); a grain-3 accreditation covers exactly this version, so a bump expires it until re-attested.

name.description: Advisory human label for the capability/offering (e.g. "EU Sovereign Compute"). uuid is authoritative.

categories.description: The `(verb × domain)` categories (ADR-PROV-002) this capability realizes, each carrying its own topology/mobility/operational/sovereignty. Two entries may share a category with different resource_types when a finer split is needed.

category_scope.description: What a capability offers for one `(verb × domain)` category — the grain at which topology/sovereignty vary (ADR-004 §2). Blocks omitted here inherit `provider_defaults`.

category.description: The `(verb × domain)` capability category, e.g. realize_resources/Compute (ADR-PROV-002).

resource_types.description: The resource types realized within this category.

conformance_claims.description: Standards/frameworks this scope DECLARES adherence to (self-declared; accreditation §3.7). Each is a claim, trusted only when an accreditation attests its framework for this scope.

topology_capability.description: The topologies this category can FULFILL (ADR-004 §1) — NOT a Topology instance (which is realized/discovered, ADR-001).

kinds_supported.description: Abstract failure/locality kinds it can guarantee separation across (region|zone|rack|host|power-domain|network-segment ...). [] = no failure-domain concept → fails any spread constraint.

jurisdictions.description: Residency/sovereignty coverage as a PLACEMENT input (where it can spread); the authorization stance is `sovereignty`.

max_separation.description: Strongest anti-affinity kind it can promise.

reference_topologies.description: Optional named topology archetypes it can fulfill.

mobility_entry.description: Data portability/migration capability for a resource type (ADR-003 §3 / ADR-004 §2).

operational_capability.description: SRE-pattern primitives (ADR-004 §3). Open (additionalProperties) so a provider can advertise more; the named ones feed fault-domain gating, rehearsal validation (T6), and rolling cutover.

sovereignty.description: The residency/sovereignty STANCE for this category (ADR-004 §4). Overrides the provider default (finest wins). This is a CLAIM: trust requires a 1-1 match with an accreditation attesting exactly this scope (accreditation §3.3.1) — declaring it here does not by itself make it trusted, and it obligates the provider to guarantee it down the whole realization pipeline for the category.

operating_jurisdictions.description: The legal SOVEREIGNTY regime(s) — matched EXACTLY (§3.8), not subsumed. ISO 3166.

data_residency_zones.description: Physical RESIDENCY zones — subsume DOWN a declared jurisdiction hierarchy (a US zone covers US-MN, §3.8).

enforcement_plane.description: Which plane the provider commits to enforce this stance on (accreditation §3.8). DCM does NOT enforce residency at the byte level — it conveys the sovereignty requirement + the role:execution data slice (contracts/data-roles.md) to the provider, and the provider's accreditation (§3.7, matching `plane`) attests it enforces at the DATA plane. The control-plane path (operator access / support / telemetry) is the universal leak; a data-only stance does not cover it.

sub_processors.description: Third parties with data access — themselves bound by the propagated sovereignty constraint (ADR-004 §4).

conformance_claim.description: A DECLARED adherence to a standard/framework — the subject's self-declaration (accreditation §3.7; Gaia-X self-description / OSCAL SSP model). A CLAIM: trusted only when an accreditation with a matching `framework` attests this scope (the same claim→attestation link as sovereignty). An accreditation ties back to it by `framework` + scope.

framework.description: The standard/framework claimed, e.g. iso_27001, soc2_type2, fedramp_moderate, gdpr, secnumcloud_3.2, c5.

level.description: Optional level/tier within the framework (e.g. Moderate, L3).

statement.description: Optional human statement of the adherence + its scope.
